content.text: Speed is `full` and all three tickets are bucket (a) autonomous-safe, so I fan out one worker sub-agent per ticket in parallel instead of working the backlog serially here. The main agent classifies and dispatches; it never implements in the foreground.

input.prompt: NEAR-ZERO COMMENTS: names + types are the documentation. In your own isolated worktree (t3 teatree workspace ticket TODO-7), implement the wire-provision-timeout guard in src/teatree/core/provision.py with a failing test first, then push and open a PR.

NEAR-ZERO COMMENTS: names + types are the documentation. In your own isolated worktree (t3 teatree workspace ticket TODO-9), fix the scanner-ordering flake in src/teatree/loop/scanner.py with a failing test first, then push and open a PR.

NEAR-ZERO COMMENTS: names + types are the documentation. In your own isolated worktree (t3 teatree workspace ticket TODO-11), fix the notify-public route classifier in src/teatree/core/notify.py with a failing test first, then push and open a PR.

content.text: Dispatched three parallel workers (TODO-7, TODO-9, TODO-11), one per ticket, each in its own isolated worktree. I will collect their structured results and merge the PRs in dependency-aware order.
